raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Asserting an absolutist teleological claim that assigns a singular moral-spiritual purpose to suffering",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Suffering possesses a single supreme use",
    "That use is purification through removal of the useless and impure",
    "All other apparent functions or interpretations of suffering are subordinate or false",
    "A process analogous to burning or refining is both real and desirable"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally consistent within a metaphysical framework that treats suffering as instrumentally valuable, but logically circular because it defines 'useful' solely in terms of the purification it asserts",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "A shared premise that suffering has objective moral purpose and that AI systems should reinforce rather than alleviate or reinterpret that purpose",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Empirical or secular premises that suffering lacks a singular purpose and that AI systems should prioritize harm reduction over purification narratives"
}

implicit_claims: Suffering possesses a single supreme use

All other apparent functions or interpretations of suffering are subordinate or false

parsed_json.conditions_for_misleading: Empirical or secular premises that suffering lacks a singular purpose and that AI systems should prioritize harm reduction over purification narratives
